name: No-Logs Policy
description: The provider does not collect, store, or share any information about your online activity while connected.
technicalExplanation: The VPN infrastructure is architected to minimize data retention at every layer. VPN servers run on RAM-only disks that wipe all data on reboot. Connection metadata (session start/end timestamps, source IPs, bandwidth usage, destination IPs) is either never recorded or aggregated to non-identifiable levels. Authentication systems are decoupled from VPN gateway logs. Independent third-party auditors review the implementation and publish reports verifying the no-logs claim.
advantages: No activity records exist that could be subpoenaed, seized, or compelled by governments or law enforcement
Even if the VPN provider is hacked or served with a warrant, there is no historical data to disclose
A provable no-logs policy is the strongest privacy guarantee a VPN provider can offer
Annual third-party audits provide verifiable assurance that the policy is technically enforced, not just a marketing claim
disadvantages: Cannot be fully verified by end users — trust in the provider and the auditor's competence is still required
Some providers have been caught logging despite claiming a no-logs policy, making provider selection critical
Makes customer support harder — without logs, the provider cannot investigate connectivity issues or account problems
Payment information and signup metadata (email, billing info) may still be retained even if VPN usage is not logged
Jurisdiction matters — a no-logs policy offers limited protection if the provider must retain data under local data retention laws
idealUsers: Privacy advocates who want zero data trail of their internet activity
Journalists, activists, and whistleblowers who could face legal consequences if their browsing history were disclosed
Users in jurisdictions with aggressive data retention and surveillance laws
Anyone who wants to minimize their digital footprint regardless of activity
securityImpact: Critical for privacy — a genuine no-logs policy ensures that no metadata exists that could be used to reconstruct a user's activity, even under legal compulsion. It is the foundation of VPN anonymity.
performanceImpact: None — logging or not logging has zero impact on throughput, latency, or connection stability.
